name: TWK10 improved muscle and functional measures in frail older adults
summary: The TWK10 trial is the narrow human functional anchor for the track baseline.
statement: In frail older adults, Lactobacillus plantarum TWK10 supplementation was associated with improvements in muscle mass, grip strength, lower-limb strength, gait speed, balance, and endurance measures over 18 weeks, especially in the high-dose group.
population_or_model: Adults aged 55 to 85 years with mild frailty
quantitative_note: The study randomized 55 participants after exclusions and reported that 17 placebo, 12 low-dose, and 13 high-dose participants completed the trial; doses were 20 billion or 60 billion CFU/day.
caveats: The trial was small and had attrition.
The result is strain-specific and should not be generalized to all live biotherapeutics or probiotics.
The publication reports multiple functional and body-composition outcomes rather than a single durable clinical endpoint.
Several authors were affiliated with the company producing the TWK10 capsules.
